notes: Single add (on-device request). The 'youngest AND oldest player to score a World Cup goal for his country' record is documented by ESPN: 'Messi is now both the youngest and the oldest Argentinean player to score at a World Cup' (2026 World Cup overreactions/takeaways). The record is per-country — Messi is NOT the youngest/oldest World Cup scorer overall — so the clue keeps 'for his country' but deliberately omits 'Argentina' to make it harder.

Secondary Wikipedia support (youngest leg + the 2026 goal): 'List of international goals scored by Lionel Messi' states he was, at 18y358d on 16 June 2006, 'the youngest-ever scorer for Argentina at a FIFA World Cup,' and lists his 2026 goal vs Algeria on 16 June 2026.

questions.clue: After scoring at the 2026 World Cup, this soccer star became both the youngest and the oldest player ever to score a World Cup goal for his country.

citations.title: World Cup 2026 overreactions and takeaways
citations.snippet: Messi is now both the youngest and the oldest Argentinean player to score at a World Cup.

citations.title: List of international goals scored by Lionel Messi
citations.snippet: His goal against Serbia and Montenegro, on 16 June 2006, at the age of 18 years and 358 days, made him the youngest-ever scorer for Argentina at a FIFA World Cup. ... 16 June 2026 — Algeria — 2026 FIFA World Cup.
